title: tax — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for the tax pipeline. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic. Callers who need tax calculation results should always go through the TaxCalculator service (which handles gate checks, retries, circuit-breaker, and snapshot persistence); helpers here are for imperative flows and support tooling.

helpers.purpose: Returns the applicable tax_rate for (jurisdiction, rate_type, applies_to, date). Reads from cache. Null when no rate exists for the tuple. Wraps TaxRateResolver::resolve() — most callers should use that directly.
Returns the most-specific matching TaxJurisdiction. Prefers city > state > country. Applies postal_code_pattern regex when set. Reads from cache.

Convenience: reads the customer's profile address (shipping preferred over billing for physical goods; billing for digital-only tenants) + delegates to resolve_tax_jurisdiction. Returns null when no address is set on the customer.

helpers.signature: resolve_active_exemptions(Tenant $tenant, string $customerType, string $customerId, TaxJurisdiction $jurisdiction, ?CarbonImmutable $on = null): Collection<TaxExemption>
helpers.purpose: Returns verified + non-expired exemptions for (tenant, customer, jurisdiction, on-date). Empty collection when none. Reads from cache.

Real-time preview WITHOUT persisting a tax_calculations row. Used by pricing pages + cart previews + POST /api/v1/tax/calculate. Applies exemptions when customer is set. Fails the same way as TaxCalculator (no silent fallback).

Combines a base rate + applicable exemptions to produce the effective percentage. Reseller exemption typically = 100% off; non-profit varies; educational = partial. Reserved for support tooling + reports; production calculations always go through the provider driver.

Returns a fresh S3 signed URL for the exemption's certificate_document_url. Default TTL 15 min. Used by SDUI view flow + support tooling. Every invocation writes an audit row (access to certificate documents is compliance-critical).
Bucketed percentage for analytics. Values: '0-5' | '5-10' | '10-20' | '20-30' | '30+'. Prevents cross-tenant tax posture inference from aggregated analytics data.
Bucketed currency amount for analytics. Values: '0' | '1-999' | '1000-9999' | '10000-99999' | '100000+'. Same rationale.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean.

The calculate_tax_preview helper is DISTINCT from the primary calculation path. Preview never persists a tax_calculations row + never blocks an invoice line. The primary path is TaxCalculator::calculate(InvoiceLine) which the invoice module invokes.
